highlights.title: Verifier-Based Reinforcement Fine-Tuning of Reasoning Models for Thermal Energy Storage Control
highlights.summary: Thermal energy storage control uses reinforcement learning
highlights.detail: The study adapts an open-weight reasoning model through reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards, achieving better results with fewer training prompts. This approach has potential for scaling across buildings.

highlights.title: Do AI Agents Know When a Task Is Simple? Toward Complexity-Aware Reasoning and Execution
highlights.summary: AI agents lack task complexity awareness
highlights.detail: The proposed E3 approach enables agents to estimate task difficulty and execute a minimum viable path, reducing unnecessary effort. This capability is crucial for efficient automation of multi-step workflows.

highlights.title: Audio-Native Speech Recognition with a Frozen Discrete-Diffusion Language Model
highlights.summary: Discrete diffusion language model for speech recognition
highlights.detail: The audio-native interface for DiffusionGemma enables parallel refinement of transcripts, offering a potential alternative to autoregressive decoders. However, training objectives may struggle to ground the audio.

highlights.title: Upcoming: Spectral::Cup 2026 Round 3 (Codeforces Round 1110, Div. 1 + Div. 2)
highlights.detail: The contest will be held on Codeforces and will consist of two divisions.

highlights.title: Guardian Angels: LLM Personalization for Productivity and Security

items.detail: This image-text-to-text model has gained substantial attention, with a large number of downloads and likes. Its capabilities and potential use cases are not detailed.

items.title: Model: InternScience/Agents-A1
items.detail: This text-generation model has been downloaded and liked by a notable number of users, although its specific features and applications are not provided.

items.title: The Linux.org story
items.detail: The story of Linux.org's founding and rebirth provides insight into the early days of Linux and its community. The site played a crucial role in helping newcomers learn about Linux.

items.title: 2026 Frikkin Lasers Challenge: Laser Bandsaw
items.detail: The challenge encourages participants to create innovative projects using lasers, such as the manually-controlled laser cutter dubbed a 'bandsaw'. The contest's specifics and rules are not detailed.

items.title: AIM-ing For a More Open Platform Than Discord
items.detail: AIM was an instant messenger that was supported for a long time, even after Discord launched.

items.title: Benchmarking Repairability Scores with an Asus Tablet
items.detail: The repairability score is based on five criteria, including documentation and availability of spare parts.

items.title: Fibrous Muscles for Humanoid Robotics
items.detail: Fibrous muscles can be used to create more human-like robots, but current robotics development is not yet at the level of Skynet.

items.title: Full Body VR Tracking Is Just Some Recycled Hardware Away
items.detail: The Vive Tracker Lite project repurposes old hardware to create a DIY full body VR tracking system.

items.title: Upcoming: Spectral::Cup 2026 Round 3 (Codeforces Round 1110, Div. 1 + Div. 2)
items.detail: The contest will be held on Codeforces and will consist of two divisions.

items.title: Building Extensible Program Logics through Effect Handlers
items.detail: The approach uses effect handlers to build extensible program logics, making it easier to develop new logics.

items.title: Cross-Core Inference Offload as an Operating-System Service on Dual-Core Microcontrollers
items.summary: Cross-core inference offload on dual-core MCUs
items.detail: The approach uses a message-based OS service to offload inference to a capable core, allowing for more efficient processing.

items.title: Inference Pipelines as Operating-System Objects: Priority Scheduling and Constant-Footprint Streaming for Microcontroller Neural Inference
items.detail: The approach makes inference pipelines a first-class OS object, allowing for more efficient and priority-based scheduling.

items.title: Quantum Weakest Preconditions Revisited: Pre-expectations for Expected Runtime Analysis
items.detail: The approach introduces a novel pre-expectation framework for analyzing the runtime behavior of quantum programs.

items.title: When is LLM-Based Program Reasoning Correct? A Completion Semantics for LLM-Based Code Inference
items.detail: The approach introduces completion semantics for LLM-based program reasoning, defining correctness relative to a completion model.

items.title: GRAFT: Graph-Matched Retrieval and Fusion of Tables in Data Lakes
items.summary: GRAFT for table retrieval and fusion
items.detail: The approach casts table retrieval as a graph matching problem and introduces a log-determinant reward for semantic relevance and evidence diversity.

items.title: ai-dynamo/dynamo
items.detail: The framework is a datacenter-scale distributed inference serving framework.

items.title: geode-sdk/geode
items.detail: The framework is designed to simplify the process of creating custom mods for Geometry Dash, a popular rhythm-based platformer game. It provides a set of tools and APIs for modders to create and share their own custom content.

items.title: google/googletest
items.detail: GoogleTest is a popular testing framework for C++ that provides a lot of features for writing and running tests. It is widely used in the industry and is known for its simplicity and flexibility.

items.title: jrouwe/JoltPhysics
items.detail: Jolt Physics is a multi-core friendly rigid body physics and collision detection library written in C++. It is suitable for games and VR applications and has been used in several high-profile games. The library is designed to be fast and efficient, making it a good choice for applications that require high-performance physics simulations.

items.title: microsoft/markitdown
items.detail: The markitdown tool is a Python script that converts files and office documents to Markdown format. It is designed to be easy to use and provides a simple way to convert documents to a format that can be easily read and edited.

items.title: sdatkinson/NeuralAmpModelerCore
items.detail: The NeuralAmpModelerCore library is a core DSP library for NAM plugins. It provides a set of functions and APIs for creating and manipulating digital signal processing models. The library is designed to be flexible and customizable, making it a good choice for applications that require advanced signal processing capabilities.
